prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of software requirements with their IDs.

Your task is to identify which of the following software requirements are non-functional requirements (NFRs).

Definition:

Functional requirements describe what the system should do, such as features, behaviors, user actions, data operations, or business rules.
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should be, perform, operate, or be constrained. They include quality attributes, system properties, technical constraints, environmental constraints, and operational constraints.

Non-functional requirements include, but are not limited to:

Performance, such as response time, throughput, scalability, capacity, concurrency, or resource usage.
Security, such as access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, integrity, or protection against malicious input.
Maintainability, such as modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, or system evolution.
Usability, such as ease of use, accessibility, learnability, user experience, or user-interface quality expectations.
Reliability, availability, robustness, or fault tolerance.
Compatibility, portability, interoperability, supported platforms, supported environments, or technology constraints.

Important guidelines:

Requirements describing constraints on supported environments, platforms, technologies, compatibility, portability, or interoperability should be considered NFRs.
Requirements about system qualities, system properties, user experience, reliability, availability, or operational constraints should be considered NFRs.
Requirements that only describe a concrete system function, user action, data operation, or business rule should be considered FRs.
If a requirement could reasonably be interpreted as either FR or NFR, classify it based on its primary concern. A behavior, feature, user action, or data operation should be classified as FR. A quality attribute, system property, or explicit constraint should be classified as NFR.
Do not classify a requirement as NFR only because it is vague or difficult to understand.

Instructions:
For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: NFR
or
REQ_ID: FR
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):
R1: NFR
R2: FR
R3: NFR
...

Requirements:
1. [R1] The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
2. [R2] The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
3. [R3] If projected  the data must be readable.  On a 10x10 projection screen  90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
4. [R4] The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC  the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
5. [R5] If projected  the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen  90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
6. [R6] The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users.  The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
7. [R7] The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory.  :  90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
8. [R8] The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
9. [R9] The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
10. [R10] The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
11. [R11] The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
12. [R12] The system shall display Events or Activities.
13. [R13] The display shall have two regions:  left 2/3 of the display is graphical  right 1/3 of the display is a data table
14. [R14] The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
15. [R15] The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
16. [R16] The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
17. [R17] The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
18. [R18] The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
19. [R19] The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
20. [R20] The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
21. [R21] The system shall allow modification of the display.
22. [R22] The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
23. [R23] The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
24. [R24] The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
25. [R25] The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
26. [R26] The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
27. [R27] The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
28. [R28] The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
